CREATE TABLE test_table (id Int32, value Nullable(Float64));
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 3.5), (3, NULL), (4, 7.2);
SELECT id, value IS NULL FROM test_table;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT id, value IS NULL FROM test_table WHERE id > 2;
CREATE TABLE other_table (ref_id Int32, ref_value Nullable(Int32));
INSERT INTO other_table VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 2), (3, NULL);
SELECT t.id, t.value IS NULL, o.ref_value IS NULL FROM test_table t JOIN other_table o ON t.id = o.ref_id;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND value IS NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE IS_NULL(value) AND id IN (SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM other_table WHERE ref_value IS NULL);
SELECT t.id, t.value IS NULL FROM test_table t LEFT JOIN other_table o ON t.id = o.ref_id WHERE o.ref_value IS NULL;
SELECT (CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'NULL' ELSE 'NOT NULL' END) as null_status FROM test_table;
SELECT id, value IS NULL FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS subquery WHERE id < 3;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (col1 Nullable(Int32), col2 Nullable(String));
SELECT col1 IS NULL, col2 IS NULL FROM complex_table;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT col1, col2 IS NULL FROM complex_table WHERE col1 IS NOT NULL) AS temp WHERE col2 IS NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL AND id IN (SELECT col1 FROM complex_table WHERE col2 IS NULL);
